methodology: Targeted web searches across IR materials, 10-Ks, sustainability reports, and industry coverage (Hydrocarbon Processing, Offshore-Technology, NS Energy, Researchandideas) for the top 20 majors. Asphalt plants, condensate splitters, and small specialty plants (<50 kbd) skipped — plant-type baseline retained. Per-grade overrides applied uniformly across the refinery's grade slate when only refinery-wide disclosures are available (typical case). Main-group yields sum to 100 by construction; merge step re-splits to leaf products via the PADD mix.
key_sources: Marathon Petroleum 2024 Annual Report / 10-K (system yield: 50.3% gasoline, 36.2% distillates, 6.5% NGL+petchem, 2.7% asphalt, 2.3% propane, 2% heavy fuel oil)
Valero Energy 2024 10-K (sales mix: 45.2% gasoline, 44.9% distillates, 9.9% other incl. petchem/coke/asphalt)
Motiva Port Arthur public profile (~40-45% gasoline, ~30-35% diesel, ~12-15% jet)
PBF Energy system-wide (48% gasoline, 33% distillates, balance jet+chemicals+asphalt+coke)
BP Cherry Point public statement (~90% gasoline+diesel+jet of throughput)
Phillips 66 site-level production rate disclosures (Bayway, Sweeny, Wood River, Lake Charles, Ponca City, Billings)
Chevron Richmond public profile (large jet share — supplies 60% of Bay Area airport jet)

refineries.audit_note: Motiva public profile + Aramco affiliate disclosures cite product mix of ~40-45% gasoline, ~30-35% diesel, ~12-15% jet, ~5-8% petrochemicals, balance LPG/coke/base-oil. Confirmed daily volumes ~265 kbd gasoline, ~140 kbd diesel, ~20 kbd jet on ~607 kbd throughput. Applied uniformly across the medium-/heavy-sour grade slate.

overrides_applied.notes: Motiva-published mix; applied to medium-sour anchor grade.
Same yield slate as Mars (analogous Gulf medium-sour assay).
Slightly heavier grade — coke share lifted, gasoline trimmed.

refineries.audit_note: Marathon Petroleum 2024 10-K system-wide yield is 50.3% gasoline, 36.2% distillates, 6.5% NGL+petchem, 2.7% asphalt, 2.3% propane, 2% heavy fuel oil. Garyville (LA, deep-conversion, NCI 12.5 + coker + hydrocracker) sits above the system average for distillates given its hydrocracker. Garyville is also Marathon's flagged target for 'shifting yields to export premium gasoline' per the 2024 Annual Report capex slate.

overrides_applied.notes: Marathon 2024 10-K system yield, tilted +0.3pp distillate vs system for Garyville hydrocracker.
Same Marathon system yield applied to medium-sour offshore grade.
Heavier offshore sour — coke share lifted, gasoline trimmed.

refineries.audit_note: Marathon Galveston Bay (Texas City) NCI 15.2, includes 29.8 kbd delayed coker + hydrocracker + FCC. Most complex Marathon site. Applied Marathon 2024 system yield with a small tilt to gasoline (Texas Gulf market) and elevated coke (NCI 15.2). Marathon 2024 10-K cited as primary source.

overrides_applied.notes: Marathon 2024 system yield + Texas City gasoline lean.
Same yield applied to medium-sour analog.

refineries.audit_note: ExxonMobil 2024 10-K + Baytown 2024 fact sheet. 588 kbd, third-largest US refinery, maximum-conversion configuration. ExxonMobil March 2025 disclosure: shifting yields across three units toward 'higher-demand lubricant base stocks and diesel fuel' away from gasoline. Baytown is one of the world's largest lube basestock plants, so lubricants share is materially above peer Gulf Coast refineries.

overrides_applied.notes: ExxonMobil 2024 Baytown fact sheet + 2024 10-K Refining segment discussion. Lubricants share material vs peer sites (one of largest base-stock plants worldwide).
Applied same slate to medium-sour analog.
Lighter offshore sour — coke trimmed, gasoline lifted modestly.

refineries.audit_note: BP Whiting reconfiguration (post-2013) supports ~4.5 billion gal/yr of transportation fuels on ~440 kbd throughput — implies ~65% of barrels going to gasoline+diesel+jet combined. Heavy WCS slate (60-70% of feed) drives coke + heavy material output. Applied a heavy-sour PADD2 deep-conversion slate.

overrides_applied.notes: Derived from BP Whiting transportation-fuels volume statement; Bakken-light fraction yields a touch more gasoline.
Heavy-sour anchor (WCS surrogate): elevated coke (~8%), depressed gasoline.
Heavier asphaltic grade — coke share above WCS proxy.

refineries.audit_note: Citgo Lake Charles deep-conversion 479 kbd. Gasoline production capacity ~209.5 kbd (~44%). Public materials cite 3.2 billion gal/yr of gasoline+diesel+jet, implying ~50-55% combined transportation share. Heavy slate (designed for Venezuelan Merey, now Iraqi Basra Heavy + Colombian Castilla) supports elevated petcoke and aromatics output.

overrides_applied.notes: Derived from Citgo-disclosed 209.5 kbd gasoline capacity on 479 kbd throughput; balance allocated to coker outputs.
Heavy Gulf-offshore sour — coke share lifted to 9% (one of highest coking capacities on Gulf).
Generic heavy import (Basrah Heavy / Castilla surrogate) — heaviest profile.

refineries.audit_note: Marathon LA (Carson/Wilmington integrated complex), 365 kbd, NCI ~13 with coker + hydrocracker. CARB gasoline + CARB diesel + LAX jet. Applied Marathon 2024 system yield with California tilt (slightly higher gasoline, lower fuel oil — California asphalt going to separate streams).

overrides_applied.notes: Marathon LA fact sheet + Marathon 2024 system yield, with California-jet uplift (LAX supply) and coker tilt.
ANS lighter than CA heavy — coke trimmed.
Asphaltic Kern — elevated bitumen + coke.

refineries.audit_note: Phillips 66 Wood River (P66/Cenovus WRB JV) 356 kbd (older 173 kbd legacy ref + WRB expansion). Site-level disclosure: 85 kbd gasoline + 70 kbd distillates daily, plus petchem feeds, asphalt, coke. Heavy Canadian sour anchor.

overrides_applied.notes: Derived from P66 Wood River disclosed 85 kbd gasoline + 70 kbd distillates on 173 kbd legacy throughput; tilted heavy for WCS.
Bakken-light run — coke share trimmed.

refineries.audit_note: Phillips 66 Bayway (Linden NJ) 258 kbd, light-low-sulfur slate. P66 disclosure: ~145 kbd gasoline + ~110 kbd distillates on 238 kbd legacy throughput, i.e. ~61% gasoline / ~46% distillates (overspec because crude swells in refining). Normalising to 100% main-group basis gives roughly 55% gasoline / 35% distillates.

overrides_applied.notes: P66 Bayway disclosed 145/110 kbd gasoline/distillates; large FCC drives high gasoline share.

refineries.audit_note: BP Cherry Point public statement: 90% of >225 kbd throughput becomes gasoline+diesel+jet. PNW jet hub (largest supplier to SEA + PDX). 7 kbd renewable diesel co-processing now also onsite. Tilted slate to jet + diesel given the PNW market.

overrides_applied.notes: BP-disclosed 90% light-products combined; PNW-jet emphasis (SEA + PDX supply).

refineries.audit_note: Chevron Richmond ~245 kbd, primary products gasoline + jet + diesel + lubricants. Public statements: supplies 60% of Bay Area airport jet, 20% of NorCal gasoline, 100% of West Coast lube oils. Implies very high jet share + meaningful lubes share, both atypical vs Gulf peers. Tilted slate accordingly.

overrides_applied.notes: Chevron Richmond product profile: high jet share (60% Bay Area airport supply) + lube basestock plant.
CA-heavy run — coke modestly elevated vs ANS.

refineries.audit_note: PBF Energy system-wide yield (per company disclosure): 48% gasoline, 33% distillates, balance jet+chemicals+asphalt+coke. Delaware City NCI 13.6, full coker + hydrocracker, designed for heavy-sour. Applied PBF system yield with elevated coke share for the heavier grades.

overrides_applied.notes: PBF Energy disclosed system mix; Delaware City heavy-sour deep-conversion config.
Heavy Canadian sour — coke + heavy material lifted.

refineries.audit_note: PBF Toledo 180 kbd, NCI 11.0 (cracking, no coker), light/sweet feed from Canada/Bakken/Midcon/Gulf. PBF system yield (48% gasoline / 33% distillates) applied — Toledo's cracking config + light-sweet feed naturally fits the high-gasoline end. No coker means petcoke share near zero; replaced with refinery gas + naphtha.

overrides_applied.notes: PBF system yield; Toledo light-sweet cracker — gasoline lifted, petcoke removed.
WTI-Cushing run — same profile as Bakken-light.

refineries.audit_note: Phillips 66 Sweeny 265 kbd, NCI 13.4, clean product yield 86%. Site disclosure: up to 140 kbd gasoline + 125 kbd distillates daily. Normalised: ~53% gasoline / ~47% distillates of total clean products, with petchem + coke for the residual ~14%.

overrides_applied.notes: P66 Sweeny disclosed 140 kbd gasoline + 125 kbd distillates (53/47 split of light products); 86% clean product yield.

refineries.audit_note: Phillips 66 Lake Charles (Westlake LA) 249 kbd. Site disclosure: up to 105 kbd gasoline + 115 kbd distillates daily; produces petcoke, propane, jet. Distillate-leaning vs Gulf peers (FCC + delayed coker + specialty coker + hydrocracker).

overrides_applied.notes: P66 Lake Charles disclosed 105 kbd gasoline + 115 kbd distillates (distillate-leaning).

refineries.audit_note: Valero Memphis 195 kbd, light-sweet feed, '~100% light products' per company disclosure (gasoline + diesel + jet + petrochemicals). Applied Valero 2024 system mix (45.2% gasoline / 44.9% distillates) with petcoke essentially eliminated.

overrides_applied.notes: Valero 2024 10-K system mix + Memphis '~100% light products' statement; no coker.
Bakken run — same light-products profile.

refineries.audit_note: Valero Benicia 170 kbd, CARBOB+CARB diesel+jet+asphalt. Valero 2024 system mix applied with California asphalt presence. Note: refinery committed to idle April 2026; baseline kept for historical scenarios.

overrides_applied.notes: Valero 2024 10-K mix + Benicia disclosed product list (CARB gasoline, diesel, jet, asphalt).

refineries.audit_note: Marathon Catlettsburg 291 kbd, deep-conversion, sweet+sour processing. Per Marathon 2024 system mix (50.3% gasoline / 36.2% distillates / 2.7% asphalt). Catlettsburg is a Marathon asphalt-producing site, justifying a small bitumen carve-out vs Marathon LA/Garyville.

overrides_applied.notes: Marathon 2024 system yield + Catlettsburg asphalt production.
WCS heavy-sour run — coke share lifted.

refineries.audit_note: ExxonMobil Joliet 275 kbd, processes Canadian heavy via Enbridge. Public statement: 11 million gallons/day of gasoline+diesel = ~262 kbd light products on 275 kbd throughput, i.e. ~95% transportation fuels. No hydrocracker (cracking config). Applied gasoline-heavy slate.

overrides_applied.notes: ExxonMobil Joliet 2024 fact sheet: 11 million gal/day gasoline+diesel from 275 kbd throughput.
Light-sweet feed — coke trimmed, gasoline lifted.

refineries.audit_note: Phillips 66 Ponca City 196-210 kbd, deep-conversion (FCC + delayed coker + hydrocracker), mixed light/medium/heavy feed via Cushing access. Anode-grade petcoke producer. Applied a typical deep-conversion Midcon yield.

overrides_applied.notes: P66 Ponca City public profile: gasoline+diesel+jet+LPG+anode coke.

refineries.audit_note: Suncor Commerce City 98 kbd: cracking config, primarily Colorado-state delivery (95% in-state). Plant 3 dedicated to asphalt — already captured in hydroskimming archetype baseline + PADD4 leaf mix. No granular yield disclosure found; baseline retained.

HF Sinclair Sinclair WY 80 kbd. 10-K cited but specific per-site yields not in available extracts; HF Sinclair system mix varies quarter to quarter per company disclosure. Baseline (cracking, PADD4, medium-sour) retained as best-available estimate.
Phillips 66 Billings 60 kbd: disclosed yields 6 kbd gasoline (10%), 4.4 kbd kerosene (7%), 30.4 kbd diesel (51%). Sharply distillate-heavy and unusual relative to peer cokers. Override applied.

overrides_applied.notes: Strongly distillate-leaning Billings yield as disclosed (6/4.4/30.4 kbd gasoline/jet/diesel of 60 kbd).
Heavy WCS run — coke share lifted, gasoline trimmed further.

refineries.audit_note: Shell Norco 227 kbd (now Valero/PBF-adjacent ownership lineage). 112 kbd RCCU (gasoline-leaning) + 40 kbd hydrocracker (diesel) + anode-coke + jet + petchem integration with Norco chemical plant + Geismar. Implies gasoline ~50%, diesel ~20%, petchem feed (ethylene/propylene precursors) noticeable.

overrides_applied.notes: Shell Norco RCCU/hydrocracker disclosure + petchem integration; elevated naphtha+LPG share for ethylene/propylene feedstock.

refineries.audit_note: Valero Sunray TX (former Diamond Shamrock). Mid-size sour-processing, no granular public yield. Valero 2024 system mix already partly captured by Memphis/Ardmore/Benicia overrides; small-share refinery — baseline retained.

Valero Three Rivers 100 kbd, produces hydrotreated naphthenic base oils alongside gasoline/diesel/jet/LPG. Specialty lube share is captured by archetype baseline. Insufficient yield disclosure for an override.

Valero Meraux LA. No public site-level yield disclosure encountered. Valero 2024 system mix (45.2% gasoline / 44.9% distillates) could be applied, but site-specific config (medium-sour, cracking-only) is already approximated by archetype baseline. Baseline retained to avoid pseudo-precision.
Valero Ardmore OK. Tier-III gasoline desulfurisation upgrade noted but no granular yield disclosure encountered. Baseline retained.

Salt Lake cluster (HF Sinclair / Marathon / Chevron / Big West, 180 kbd combined): four small hydroskimming sites with diverse operators. No combined yield disclosure exists. Hydroskimming + PADD4 baseline retained.
Petromax Houston condensate splitter: condensate-splitter archetype baseline is adequate; the unit physically cannot produce coker products. No public yield disclosure exists. Baseline retained.
TX International Terminals Galveston condensate splitter — same logic as Petromax. Baseline retained.
Placid LA Port Allen: small specialty plant; no public yield disclosure. Baseline retained.
United Refining Warren PA: small specialty plant, mostly captive lubricants + asphalt + gasoline + diesel for regional market. No granular disclosure. Baseline retained.
San Joaquin Bakersfield: small CA specialty plant. Baseline retained.
Silver Eagle Evanston WY: very small (<10 kbd) specialty plant. Baseline retained.
San Antonio Refinery small specialty plant. Baseline retained.
Asphalt plant — baseline plant_type template adequate; no public yield data exists. Baseline retained.

Ultramar CA Wilmington (Valero-affiliated heritage): small specialty CA plant. Valero 2024 system mix would cover but site is too small to merit refinery-specific override beyond archetype. Baseline retained.
Asphalt plant — baseline plant_type template adequate; no public yield data exists. Baseline retained.
